questions.question: Why does the standard ViT block use pre-LayerNorm placement instead of post-LayerNorm?
options: Pre-LN trains stably at depth without learning-rate warm-up while post-LN diverges past about 6 layers
questions.explanation: Pre-LN normalizes each sub-layer's input which keeps gradient norms bounded as depth grows.

questions.question: At hidden=768 with heads=12, what is the per-head dimension and why does it matter?
options: 12, so each head can run on a small GPU
64, which controls the resolution of each attention map and lets twelve heads attend independently in parallel
768, because all heads share the projection
questions.explanation: head_dim = hidden / heads = 64. Each head projects the token to its own 64-D q/k/v and computes its own attention map.

questions.question: Does the ViT encoder need a causal attention mask?
options: No: the encoder is bidirectional, every token may attend to every other token in the same sequence
questions.explanation: Vision encoders are bidirectional. Causal masking appears later when a text decoder consumes the cross-attention output.

questions.question: What does the CLS token's final hidden state represent after all 12 blocks?
options: A copy of the input embedding
A learned vector summary of the entire image that downstream heads project into class logits or contrastive embeddings
questions.explanation: The CLS token starts empty and accumulates information from every patch through self-attention across all 12 blocks.

questions.question: Why is the feed-forward layer expanded to 4x the hidden dim?
options: An empirical sweet spot since the original Transformer; smaller underfits, larger overfits at fixed data budget
It removes the need for attention
questions.explanation: The 4x factor has held across language and vision transformers since 2017. Capacity per parameter peaks here.

questions.question: Which family extension keeps the block math unchanged but adds prepended learned tokens?
options: Register tokens (DINOv2): a few learned vectors prepended after CLS that capture global statistics and smooth attention maps
questions.explanation: Register tokens stabilize attention without altering the block's math; they are extra slots in the sequence.
